findings.observation: Implemented explicit conflict resolution strategy (skip/overwrite/rename) in batch creation, reducing risk of accidental data loss.
findings.recommendation: Ensure 'rename' logic handles potential collisions with existing entities comprehensively.

findings.observation: Hello_World acts as a functional boilerplate, consistently structured with registration, input/output schema, and meta annotations.
findings.recommendation: Maintain this structure as the standard template for new abilities.

findings.observation: No SAST configuration files (phpstan.neon, psalm.xml, .semgrep.yml) found in the project root.
findings.evidence: SAST config check results — none found
findings.recommendation: Integrate a standardized static analysis tool with appropriate configuration files to ensure code quality. Recommended: Psalm with --taint-analysis for XSS/SQLi detection.

findings.observation: No ESLint or related front-end linting configurations detected.
findings.evidence: SAST config check results — none found
findings.recommendation: Implement ESLint with predefined rulesets (incl. eslint-plugin-security) for standardized JavaScript/TypeScript quality metrics.

findings.observation: Registry methods and meta-annotation patterns are consistent across audit classes, ensuring uniform ability registration.
findings.recommendation: Ensure return formats in output_schema remain strictly typed and consistent across all audit suite modules.

findings.observation: Uses a comprehensive blocklist of dangerous functions coupled with token parsing as a primary security layer.
findings.recommendation: Supplement the function blocklist with an allowlist approach for higher sensitivity environments, acknowledging the inherent limitations of static analysis for remote code execution prevention.
